title: TDS AI OS — Live Task Ledger
description: The authoritative, resume-anchoring task ledger for the TDS repository. Read alongside SESSION_SUMMARY.md at the start of every session to rehydrate state after context loss. Reconciled against the actual repo (npm scripts, generated outputs, docs/ai-os/*, .ai/*). Backend/DB/API/CI/test-framework tasks are PLANNED — none of that infrastructure exists in the repo today.
precedence: Governed by docs/ai-os/29_TASK_EXECUTION.md and docs/ai-os/28_RESUME_SPECIFICATION.md. Design Lock (00_MASTER_RULES.md) is never overridden by any task here.

taskSchema.id: string — stable unique id, format 'T-NNN'. Never reused, never renumbered.
taskSchema.title: string — imperative one-line summary of the deliverable.
taskSchema.status: enum — one of statuses[]: todo | in_progress | blocked | review | done.
taskSchema.owner: string — responsible agent or role; one of owners[].
taskSchema.phase: enum — one of phases[]: built | ai-os | backend-planned | ci-planned | testing-planned.
taskSchema.planned: boolean — true when the task targets infrastructure that DOES NOT exist in the repo yet.
taskSchema.gates: string[] — validation gates that MUST pass before status can become 'done'; drawn from gates[].
taskSchema.dependsOn: string[] — task ids that MUST reach 'done' (or 'review') before this task can start.
taskSchema.blockedBy: string[] — reason strings or task ids currently preventing progress (present when status = blocked).
taskSchema.checkpoints: checkpoint[] — ordered subtask progress markers; the resume granularity below task level.
taskSchema.artifacts: string[] — repo-relative paths this task creates or maintains (source of truth for 'is it done').
taskSchema.acceptance: string — the single, testable condition that defines completion.
taskSchema.notes: string — optional free-form context, escalations, or Design-Lock reminders.
taskSchema.updatedAt: string — ISO 8601 or 'SEED'; bump on every status/checkpoint change.

checkpointSchema.label: string — short description of the sub-step.

gates.lint: npm run lint — eslint . passes with zero errors.
gates.typecheck: tsc -b (or plugin:typecheck) passes with no type errors.
gates.ds:build: npm run ds:build — tokens + manifest + catalog regenerate cleanly.
gates.figma:build: npm run figma:build — ds:build + plugin typecheck + plugin bundle + headless harness all green.
gates.plugin:test: npm run plugin:test — figma/plugin/test/harness.ts asserts full coverage, exits 0.
gates.manifest-schema-valid: Emitted .ai/*.json parse as valid JSON and satisfy 27_MANIFEST_SPECIFICATION.md.
gates.design-lock: No unrequested visual change; Storybook remains the immutable source of truth (00_MASTER_RULES.md).
gates.format: npm run format:check — prettier reports no diffs.

policy.resume: On session start: read SESSION_SUMMARY.md, then this file. Pick the first task with status in_progress; if none, the first todo whose dependsOn are all done. Resume at the first incomplete checkpoint. See 28_RESUME_SPECIFICATION.md.
policy.idempotency: Re-running a task's build gate (ds:build/figma:build) MUST be safe and reproducible; generated outputs are deterministic.
policy.doneDefinition: A task is 'done' only when every gate in gates[] passes AND every artifact in artifacts[] exists. Otherwise it stays in_progress/review.
policy.updateRule: Any status or checkpoint change MUST bump updatedAt and be reflected in SESSION_SUMMARY.md and CHANGELOG.md.

tasks.title: Build the 60-component metadata-driven design system (24 atoms, 27 molecules, 9 organisms)
tasks.acceptance: All 60 components export from @/components, each with a pure-data X.meta.ts driving React + Storybook + manifest.
tasks.notes: Baseline reality. Most future work is REUSE (docs/COMPONENTS.md), not new components.

tasks.title: Author the token system — 3 collections (Primitives, Semantic, Theme light/dark) + effect/text styles
tasks.acceptance: npm run tokens:build regenerates all three generated token files; all component CSS references only var(--tds-*).
tasks.notes: Generated token files are SACRED — edit source in src/tokens/*, never the generated output.

tasks.title: Stand up Storybook 8 as the immutable source of truth (CSF3 stories, autodocs, a11y, interactions)
tasks.acceptance: npm run storybook renders all 60 components with controls derived from each meta; Storybook is the visual reference.
tasks.notes: Design Lock: Storybook is IMMUTABLE for visual design. Figma reproduces it, never redesigns it.

tasks.title: Generate the Figma bundle contract — figma/tds.plugin.json { tokens, design } + design-system.manifest.json

checkpoints.label: scripts/build-manifest.ts + scripts/lib/css-bindings.ts derive per-variant token bindings

tasks.acceptance: npm run manifest:build emits the { $schema, version, generator, tokens, design } bundle covering all 60 component sets.
tasks.notes: Bundle shape is the contract in figma/README.md. State axis intentionally excluded from Figma variants.

tasks.title: Build the Figma plugin that regenerates the system from the bundle + its headless coverage harness
tasks.acceptance: npm run figma:build completes with the headless harness passing (no per-component code; entry is code.ts).
tasks.notes: 8 auto-generated Pages. No networkAccess. Faithful reproduction only — never invent/rename/simplify.

tasks.title: Generate the reuse-first component catalog docs/COMPONENTS.md
tasks.acceptance: npm run catalog:build produces a full index (variants, props, one-line purpose) for all 60 components.
tasks.notes: Catalog-first reading minimizes AI tokens (26_TOKEN_OPTIMIZATION.md). Regenerate after any component change.

tasks.title: Author the 41 AI OS specification documents under docs/ai-os/
tasks.acceptance: All 41 files 00-40 exist, each following the mandatory 12-section structure with correct front-matter and cross-links.
tasks.notes: DONE. All 41 docs (00-40) present + README.md + _VALIDATION_REPORT.md; each verified to carry the 12-section structure. Capstone authored on the main thread after the generation workflow hit a session limit.

tasks.title: Author the 17 runtime manifests under .ai/
tasks.acceptance: All 17 manifests exist, parse as valid JSON/MD, carry $schema/version/generatedAt, and satisfy 27_MANIFEST_SPECIFICATION.md.
tasks.notes: DONE. All 17 manifests present; all 14 JSON parse cleanly. The 8 data-derived ones are regenerated by scripts/build-ai-manifests.ts (T-015); the 9 seed/planned ones are hand-authored and left untouched by the generator.

tasks.title: Implement scripts/build-ai-manifests.ts to auto-derive data-backed .ai manifests from source

checkpoints.label: Wire into an ai:manifests npm script; regenerate as part of ds:build

tasks.acceptance: A single npm script regenerates all data-derived .ai manifests deterministically from src sources + generated outputs.
tasks.notes: DONE + verified. npm run ai:manifests regenerates the 8 data-derived manifests (60-source view + 96-Figma-entry view surfaced without contradiction) and leaves the 9 seed/planned files untouched; chained into ds:build. ds:build and figma:build both green.

tasks.title: PLANNED — Stand up Supabase backend (Postgres + Auth + RLS + Storage + Edge Functions + Realtime)

checkpoints.label: Provision Supabase project + local supabase/ dir + migrations

tasks.acceptance: A Supabase backend engineered to scale beyond 10,000,000 users exists and matches 17_SUPABASE_GUIDE.md.
tasks.notes: STATUS: PLANNED — NO backend, database, auth, API, or secrets exist in the repo today. Runtime deps are only react + react-dom.

tasks.title: PLANNED — Design the Postgres schema, migrations, indexing + emit .ai/ERD.json
tasks.acceptance: ERD.json reflects a live, migration-backed schema consistent with 14_DATABASE_GUIDE.md.
tasks.notes: STATUS: PLANNED. ERD.json ships seeded with status:planned until the schema exists.

tasks.title: PLANNED — Build the Node/TS API layer over Supabase + emit .ai/API_SPEC.json
tasks.acceptance: A versioned API layer exists with API_SPEC.json as its contract, matching 15_API_GUIDE.md + 16_NODE_GUIDE.md.
tasks.notes: STATUS: PLANNED. Frontend/Backend/Database sync is a target, not current reality.

tasks.title: PLANNED — Add CI/CD (.github/workflows) gating lint, typecheck, ds:build, figma:build, plugin:test

checkpoints.label: PR workflow: lint + build + figma:build + plugin:test

tasks.acceptance: Every PR is gated in CI by the same commands the AI OS enforces locally; drift and design-lock checks run automatically.
tasks.notes: STATUS: PLANNED — no .github/ directory or CI exists today.

tasks.title: PLANNED — Add a test framework (unit + interaction + visual + e2e) beyond the headless plugin harness

checkpoints.label: Choose + wire runner (e.g. Vitest) + a 'test' npm script
e2e for PLANNED backend flows; coverage gates per 24_TESTING_GUIDE.md

tasks.acceptance: A test pyramid exists with coverage gates; the existing figma/plugin/test/harness.ts remains the plugin coverage gate.
tasks.notes: STATUS: PLANNED — today there is NO vitest/playwright/jest config, no *.test/*.spec files, and no 'test' npm script.

tasks.title: PLANNED — Stand up MCP tool surface for manifest read/update, session recovery, and Storybook<->Figma sync

checkpoints.label: Read/update .ai manifests + recover sessions from SESSION_SUMMARY.md + TASKS.json

tasks.acceptance: MCP agents can read/update manifests, resume sessions, and verify Storybook<->Figma sync with minimal tokens.
tasks.notes: STATUS: PLANNED. Depends on the .ai manifest set and its regeneration script.

validation: File MUST parse as valid JSON.
Every task.id MUST be unique and match /^T-\d{3}$/.
Every task.status MUST be one of statuses[]; every task.owner one of owners[]; every task.phase one of phases[].
Every dependsOn id MUST reference an existing task.id.
counts MUST equal the tallies of tasks[] by status.
A task MAY be 'done' only if all its gates are known keys of gates{} and its artifacts[] exist in the repo.
Backend/DB/API/CI/test tasks MUST carry planned:true and a phase ending in '-planned'.
